markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Create backend-visible repair queue for failed extraction, missing assets, denied paths, and stale hashes. This MT belongs to SourceIngestionAndEvidence. Ingest project, governance, media, and research sources with evidence and failure receipts.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Source ingestion must be allowlisted, hash-based, restartable, and secret-aware.
PDF/media extraction failures must produce receipts and repairable metadata rather than silently indexing partial content.
scope.expected_behavior: SourceRepairQueue leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: SourceRepairQueue is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.implementation_evidence: wtc-kernel-009 feat/WP-KERNEL-009 e3e154b4; real-PG tests green (group total 80 passed/0 failed; lopdf 0.41 MIT only dep added). Pending adversarial code review before READY_FOR_VALIDATION.

concerns: BUDGET BOUNDED, NO INFINITE LOOP: begin_repair_attempt only claims when attempts < max_attempts (default 3); on exhaustion it dead-letters and returns a typed Conflict (no 4th attempt). Terminal states (resolved, dead_letter) refuse further transitions (is_terminal guard + optimistic WHERE state='retrying'/state IN open). Verified by lifecycle mt094 (3 attempts -> dead_letter -> 'terminal' conflict) and resolve path. TERMINAL STAYS TERMINAL: CONFIRMED.

DEAD-LETTER ROW-GROWTH (the across-pass gap, not refuted by tests): the partial unique index uq_*_repair_open_source covers only state IN (queued, retrying), so it guarantees at most ONE OPEN entry per source — but a DEAD-LETTERED entry is NOT covered. enqueue_repair's UPDATE matches only open states, so once an entry dead-letters, the NEXT ingestion pass over the same still-failing file INSERTs a FRESH queued entry (attempts=0). Across N passes of a permanently-failing-and-dead-lettered file you accumulate N repair rows (1 dead_letter + new queued each cycle). The test mt094_refailing_source...multiplying_rows proves the OPEN-entry dedup (3 passes -> 1 open) but does NOT exercise pass-after-dead-letter, so the growth is real though bounded per pass. HARDENING: on enqueue, if a dead_letter entry exists for the source with the same reason_class, refresh/reopen it under operator control rather than inserting a new row, or require explicit operator reopen.

MISSING-ASSET handling: a vanished file during retry settles FailedAgain/MISSING_ASSET and requeues (attempts counted) rather than panicking — verified mt094 dlq test. Partial retry RESOLVES (residual loss documented) to avoid looping forever on identical content — sensible.

is_repairable() correctly EXCLUDES SecretBlocked and UnsupportedFormat, so a blocked secret file and an unsupported binary never enter the retry queue (verified fixture mt096: secret + blob NOT queued).
evasion_probes: attempts exhaust -> dead_letter -> typed 'terminal' conflict, never a 4th attempt CONFIRMED.
3 passes over a perma-failing file -> exactly 1 OPEN entry (UPDATE-in-place) CONFIRMED.
missing asset on retry -> requeue + MISSING_ASSET reason, no panic CONFIRMED.
resolved entry must name resolved_receipt_id (DB chk_*_resolved_shape) CONFIRMED.
PASS-AFTER-DEAD-LETTER -> new queued row inserted (row growth) — NOT covered by any test; flagged above.
reason vocabulary superset (extraction classes + MISSING_ASSET/DENIED_PATH/STALE_HASH) round-trips CONFIRMED.
evidence_checked: store.rs:415-634 enqueue_repair (UPDATE-open-first then INSERT), begin_repair_attempt (budget guard + dead-letter), settle_repair_attempt, dead_letter_repair
migrations/0164_knowledge_ingestion_repair_queue.sql (partial unique index on open states only; max_attempts>=1; resolved-shape CHECK)
tests/knowledge_ingestion_lifecycle_tests.rs mt094 resolve/dead-letter/dedupe + fixture mt096 queue population

hardening_pending.reason: Dead-letter row growth across passes (partial unique index covers only open states). Reopen existing dead-letter row instead of inserting.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
